desc:
In every direction you look, an endless field extends, covered by green and
thick grass. Lonely trees can be seen in different places and wild flowers of
mixed colours give some life to this land.
name: Green Fields

desc:
To the north you can see a big field of thick grass and flowers. It seems
endless. To the south you see the road going to Stock, a small hobbit village
located just to the south-east of here.
name: Green Fields

desc:
You are walking in the fields just north of Stock. Wheat seems to be the primary
plant here, but you can spot some barley here and there as well. The path you
make through the fields makes your track very obvious, maybe you should get out
of the fields before some angry farmer arrives.
name: Fields of Stock

desc:
You are walking in the middle of a huge wheat field, obviously trying to destroy
as much as you can of a sorry farmer's crop. A farm is east of here, behind a
small fence. You can see the road some distance to the north, beyond a green
field.
name: Wheat Field

desc:
You are standing in the Village of Stock, one of the larger hobbit villages. To
the north you see the famous Golden Perch, famous for the best beer in the East
Farthing. The road splits here, and you can continue west, south and east.
name: Stock

desc:
The village of Stock is just to the west, whilst to the south a bridge over the
Stockbrook leads to the Causeway. The Brandywine still runs to the east, soon to
find its bank. Following the long path north, the Brandywine Bridge awaits.
name: Path to Stock

desc:
You are paddling slowly on the River Brandywine, which is slowly growing bigger
on its way to the southwest. The river is now well inside the borders of the
Shire, and it is indeed a peaceful place. Green fields and small forests border
the river on both sides, and small rings on the water show that there are fish
in the river as well. You can climb the bank to the west, where you can see
Stock in the distance.
name: River Brandywine

desc:
This wide heavily used path leads south to the village of Stock, whilst
northwards the East Road can be found. The Brandywine runs just east of here,
nourishing the wheat fields to the west.
name: Path to Stock

desc:
Far to the west you can find some wheat fields, and Stock is yet a little to the
southwest. The path north takes travellers to the East Road, close to the
Brandywine Bridge.
name: Path to Stock

desc:
Whoever maintains this huge garden beside the farm takes great care and pride in
its cultivation. There are beautiful flowers and plants neatly arranged in rows
and other patterns. The sweet smell of flowers often fills the air and the
bloom-laiden plants sway in the spring breezes. A cute little fence blocks the
way in all directions but to the north.
name: In a Huge Garden

desc:
The road goes ever on, yet the local wildlife to the west draws attention whilst
the fish to the east swim by undisturbed. The Old Forest is in sight beyond the
Brandywine, far off, but still it gives fright.
name: Path to Stock

desc:
The path continues to the east and west, and as ever the Brandywine travels with
it to the south. The road is well travelled and from time to time, a hobbit or
two passes to do daily labours.
name: Path to Stock

desc:
To the north the East Road awaits, whilst south the path leads to the village of
Stock, yet it is still far off. The Brandywine River flows east, but entering it
seems impossible.
name: Path to Stock

desc:
You are paddling slowly on the River Brandywine, which is slowly growing bigger
on its way to the southwest. The river is now well inside the borders of the
Shire, and it is indeed a peaceful place. Green fields and small forests border
the river on both sides, and small rings on the water show that there are fish
in the river as well.
name: River Brandywine

desc:
You are paddling slowly on the River Brandywine, which is slowly growing bigger
on its way to the southwest. The river is now well inside the borders of the
Shire, and it is indeed a peaceful place. Green fields and small forests border
the river on both sides, and small rings on the water show that there are fish
in the river as well.
name: River Brandywine

desc:
You are paddling slowly on the River Brandywine, which is slowly growing bigger
on its way to the southwest. The river is now well inside the borders of the
Shire, and it is indeed a peaceful place. Green fields and small forests border
the river on both sides, and small rings on the water show that there are fish
in the river as well.
name: River Brandywine

desc:
You are paddling slowly on the River Brandywine, which is slowly growing bigger
on its way to the southwest. The river is now well inside the borders of the
Shire, and it is indeed a peaceful place. Green fields and small forests border
the river on both sides, and small rings on the water show that there are fish
in the river as well.
name: River Brandywine

desc:
You are paddling slowly on the River Brandywine, which is slowly growing bigger
on its way to the southwest. The river is now well inside the borders of the
Shire, and it is indeed a peaceful place. Green fields and small forests border
the river on both sides, and small rings on the water show that there are fish
in the river as well.
name: River Brandywine

desc:
As you travel on the River Brandywine, you see a stream to the west. Across it
stands a bridge. Just north of the bridge lies the village of Stock. The
Brandywine River runs from the north to the south. To the south you can enter
the Ferry. East of here you can follow a path southward and enter Bucklebury.
name: Fork in the River

desc:
You are on a small river called Stockbrook, just south of Stock. East of you,
behind a bridge, you see that this river runs into the larger Brandywine river.
You look southwest and see a large area named the Marish.
name: Stockbrook

desc:
The bridge of the Causeway over Stockbrook is not far to the east, and behind it
the waters of the Stockbrook join those of the mighty Baranduin on their way to
the ocean far in the south.
name: The Stockbrook

desc:
You are on the Brandywine, between the Buckland and the Marish. South of you,
you see that the river bends to the west.
name: Brandywine

desc:
You are on the Brandywine, between the Buckland and the Marish. The river bends
to the west and to the north here. Along the river you see a road. It seems to
go between Bucklebury and Standelf.
name: Brandywine

desc:
You are on the Brandywine, between the Buckland and the Marish. Just south of
you is the village of Standelf.
name: Brandywine

desc:
The Brandywine flows from east to west for a short time before bending back on
its way south to the ocean. This is a slow flowing, large river with plenty of
fish. On the northern bank is the Causeway that follows the river, to the south
are the well-tended fields of Buckland.
name: Brandywine

desc:
You are on the peaceful Brandywine river. Here, the river forms a graceful bend
from the east to the south. North and west of you, is the Causeway and dry land.
name: Bend in the Brandywine

desc:
You are floating in the middle of the Brandywine river. The river flows from
north to south here. To the west is the Causeway. Beyond the road, you see the
forest of Woody End.
name: The Brandywine

desc:
You are on the Brandywine river. The river bends gently from north to west here.
To the northwest, you can see the road called the Causeway. Beyond the road, you
see the Woody End forest. Not far over the hills to the east is the village of
Standelf.
name: Bend in the Brandywine

desc:
You are walking on a road through the Marish. The road goes southward from here.
To the east is the Bucklebury Ferry. It's self-operated: If you board the Ferry
you'll have to use a pole to get across.
name: The Causeway

desc:
You are standing in a fertile and boggy area. A few hobbits seem to still live
here but most have probably moved to Buckland. Across a stream to the north, you
see a village.
name: The Marish

desc:
This is the road from Bucklebury to Standelf. West of you is the Ferry and east
of you are large fields of wheat. The Ferry is self-operated: If you board it,
you'll have to use a pole to push it across the river.
name: Road to Standelf

desc:
You are standing in a fertile, yet boggy, area. You can see many deep Hobbit
footprints in the soft soil. While a few Hobbits might still live here, most
have probably moved to Buckland. Across a brook to the north, you see a village.
name: The Marish

desc:
You are standing in a fertile, yet boggy, area. You can see many deep Hobbit
foot prints in the soft soil. These wetlands are very difficult to move in. To
the south, there is a solid road which will make your journey much easier.
name: The Marish

desc:
You are on a road travelling through the Marish alongside the Brandywine River.
A few hobbit dwellings are on the side of the road, but they appear to be mostly
abandoned. The road continues east and west with the Brandywine to the south,
and the Marish stretching off to the north.
name: The Causeway

desc:
You are walking on a road through the Marish. A few hobbit dwellings are on the
side of the road, but they are mostly abandoned.
name: The Causeway

desc:
You are walking on a road through the Marish. A few hobbit dwellings are on the
side of the road, but they are mostly abandoned.
name: The Causeway

desc:
You are standing on a bridge across a stream called Stockbrook. To the east you
can see the stream go into River Baranduin, or the Brandywine. A few hundred
yards north of you, you see the village of Stock.
name: Bridge across Stockbrook

desc:
You are standing in the chief village of Buckland. Beneath a hill called Buck
Hill you see a large smial, the Brandy Hall. This is the chief dwelling of the
Brandybucks, one of the most important Hobbit families of the Shire.
name: Bucklebury

desc:
You are walking on a road through the fields of Buckland. Brandywine River
floats a few hundred yards west of here. To the west you can enter Bucklebury,
the chief village of Buckland. The road goes a few hundred yards east of here
and then bends to the north.
name: Dusty Road

desc:
You are walking on a road through the fields of Buckland. The road goes to the
north and to the west here. East of here is Crickhollow, one of the houses of
the Brandybucks.
name: Dusty Road

desc:
You are walking on a dusty road, which extends further south than you can see.
The Brandywine is west of here, and the hedge is to the east. On the other side
of the river you can see vast green fields, but a dark forest is looming beyond
the hedge.
name: Dusty Road

desc:
You are walking on a dusty road, which extends further south than you can see.
The Brandywine is west of here, and the hedge is to the east. On the other side
of the river you can see vast green fields, but a dark forest is looming beyond
the hedge. The road continues north and west.
name: Dusty Road

desc:
You are walking on a dusty road, which extends further south than you can see.
The Brandywine is west of here, and the hedge is to the east. On the other side
of the river you can see vast green fields, but a dark forest is looming beyond
the hedge. The road continues north and south.
name: Dusty Road

desc:
You are walking on a dusty road, which extends further south than you can see.
The Brandywine is west of here, and the hedge is to the east. On the other side
of the river you can see vast green fields, but a dark forest is looming beyond
the hedge. A small path leads east to the village of Newbury.
name: Dusty Road

desc:
This is the road from Bucklebury to Standelf. West of you is the Brandywine and
east of you are large fields of wheat.
name: Road to Standelf

desc:
This is the road from Bucklebury to Standelf. West of you is the Brandywine and
east of you is a small hobbit farm.
name: Road to Standelf

desc:
This is the road from Bucklebury to Standelf. West of you is the Brandywine and
east of you are large fields of wheat.
name: Road to Standelf

desc:
This is the road from Bucklebury to Standelf. North of you is the Brandywine and
westward you see the hobbit village of Standelf.
name: Road to Standelf

desc:
You are in a village in southern Buckland. The houses are all in the fashion
hobbits like them; round windows and round doors. Some of the houses are built
partially or completely into the green hill sides.
name: Standelf

desc:
You walk along the bank of the Brandywine river. Across the river you see the
Marish. On this side of the river, numerous large fields are seen with a few
farms spread around. Just east of you is the village of Standelf, the home for a
hundred hobbits or so.
name: At the Bank of the Brandywine River

desc:
Large fields are everywhere only separated by hedges and lines of trees. You are
now in the westmost part of Buckland. Standelf is northeast of you, and Rushey
is to the southwest.
name: Fields

desc:
You are in some fields just south of the village of Standelf. The air is clear
and fresh, and you you find it quite refreshing to walk around here, flattening
the wheat of the local farmers.
name: Fields

desc:
It must have been a long time ago since some farmer tried to cultivate this
area. Right east of you is the Hay, and behind it there is a dark forest. Maybe
the farmer feared the Forest and therefore decided to abandon this place?
name: Abandoned Field

desc:
You see a long hedge going east and west separating Buckland and the Old Forest.
The hedge was built by the Brandybucks as a defence against the evil of the
Forest. It is said that a long time ago the trees of this forest attacked
Buckland. Further east, the Hay is bending northward. Further west, the Hay is
bending southward.
name: The High Hay

desc:
You are in the middle of a field. You see nothing special here. Maybe you should
rather walk on the road just north of you instead of damaging the wheat in this
field. Standelf is located a few hundred yards west of you.
name: Fields

desc:
You are just south of a road, in the middle of some fields. You see some farms
nearby; one only a hundred yards east of here. Maybe you should stay away from
that farm; the farmer wouldn't be pleased to see you walking around in his
fields.
name: Fields

desc:
You see a long hedge going far from the north and continuing southward. It is
separating Buckland and the Old Forest. The hedge was built by the Brandybucks
as a defence against the evil of the Forest. It is said that a long time ago the
trees of this forest attacked Buckland. The Hay makes a bend in this area. You
see it go on to the south and to the west.
name: The High Hay

desc:
You see a long hedge going north and south separating Buckland and the Old
Forest. The hedge was probably built by the Brandybucks as a defence against the
evil of the Forest. It is said that a long time ago the trees of this forest
attacked Buckland.
name: The High Hay

desc:
You see a long hedge going north and west separating Buckland and the Old
Forest. The hedge was built by the Brandybucks as a defence against the evil of
the Forest. It is said that a long time ago the trees of this forest attacked
Buckland. There is a sign here.
name: The High Hay

desc:
You see a long hedge going north and south separating Buckland and the Old
Forest. The hedge was built by the Brandybucks as a defence against the evil of
the Forest. It is said that a long time ago the trees of this forest attacked
Buckland.
name: The High Hay

desc:
You see a long hedge going north and south separating Buckland and the Old
Forest. The hedge was built by the Brandybucks as a defence against the evil of
the Forest. It is said that a long time ago the trees of this forest attacked
Buckland.
name: The High Hay

desc:
You see a long hedge going north and south separating Buckland and the Old
Forest. The hedge was built by the Brandybucks as a defence against the evil of
the Forest. It is said that a long time ago the trees of this forest attacked
Buckland. Right here, the Hay bends. You can follow it to the east or to the
south.
name: The High Hay

desc:
You see a long hedge going north and south separating Buckland and the Old
Forest. The hedge was built by the Brandybucks as a defence against the evil of
the Forest. It is said that a long time ago the trees of this forest attacked
Buckland. Right here, the hay bends, and you can follow it northward or
westward.
name: The High Hay

desc:
You see a long hedge going north and south separating Buckland and the Old
Forest. The hedge was built by the Brandybucks as a defence against the evil of
the Forest. It is said that a long time ago the trees of this forest attacked
Buckland. You notice a sign. Something important must have been written on it.
name: The High Hay

desc:
You found another small hobbit farm in the middle of the fields of Buckland. You
see a small path going north leading to a small farm far away.
name: Another Small Hobbit Farm

desc:
This is a small path going north and south between two hobbit farms. If you
cross a small belt of wheat to the west, you will find yourself on a larger
road. You are very close to the Forest here, but the High Hay offers you
protection against it.
name: A Small Path through the Field

desc:
You are in a bend in a hedge. You see a long hedge going far north and far south
separating Buckland and the Old Forest. The hedge was probably built by the
Brandybucks as a defence against the evil of the Forest. It is said that a long
time ago the trees of this forest attacked Buckland.
name: The High Hay

desc:
You see a long hedge going north and south separating Buckland and the Old
Forest. The hedge was built by the Brandybucks as a defence against the evil of
the Forest. It is said that a long time ago the trees of this forest attacked
Buckland. The Hay thickens here, and it is even taller. It is impossible to
climb over it. If you really want to enter the Forest, you should follow the Hay
either to the south or the north. Maybe the Hay offers a way through itself
somewhere else.
name: The High Hay

desc:
You see a long hedge going north and south separating Buckland and the Old
Forest. The hedge was built by the Brandybucks as a defence against the evil of
the Forest. It is said that a long time ago the trees of this forest attacked
Buckland. The Hay seems to go on into infinity both to the south and to the
north. A sign is posted here.
name: The High Hay

desc:
You make your way through the wheat, and you have now reached the end of the
fields. Close to you, is a hedge going north-south, bending slightly east-west.
There is a farm to the west.
name: Fields

desc:
This is a small, quiet hobbit farm. It is surrounded by large fields of wheat. A
small road goes to the west to a larger road. In a distance, you can see the
village of Standelf.
name: A Small Hobbit Farm

desc:
For some reason you walk in a field, flattening the wheat, instead of walking on
the road just west of you. A small farm is to the south.
name: Fields

desc:
You walk around in the fields of Buckland. Fields of wheat are everywhere, only
separated by a few hedges. You spot a few farms in a distance.
name: Fields

desc:
You see a long hedge going far north and far south separating Buckland and the
Old Forest. The hedge was built by the Brandybucks as a defence against the evil
of the Forest. It is said that a long time ago the trees of this forest attacked
Buckland. You can follow the Hay, or you can enter the fields west of here. You
can't get across the Hay from this place.
name: The High Hay

desc:
You see a long hedge going north and south separating Buckland and the Old
Forest. The hedge was built by the Brandybucks as a defence against the evil of
the Forest. It is said that a long time ago the trees of this forest attacked
Buckland. The Hay bends slightly here. It goes south-west and north-east. Fields
are seen west of you, and the Forest is seen east of here, behind the Hay. You
spot a possible way through the Hay into the Forest some hundred yards northeast
of here.
name: The High Hay

desc:
You see a long hedge going north and south separating Buckland and the Old
Forest. The hedge was built by the Brandybucks as a defence against the evil of
the Forest. It is said that a long time ago the trees of this forest attacked
Buckland. It is possible to climb the Hay and enter the Forest, but you doubt it
would be a very good idea. There is a sign mounted in the ground.
name: The High Hay

desc:
You see a long hedge going north and south separating Buckland and the Old
Forest. The hedge was built by the Brandybucks as a defence against the evil of
the Forest. It is said that a long time ago the trees of this forest attacked
Buckland. You spot a possible way through the Hay into the Forest a few hundred
yards east of you.
name: The High Hay

desc:
You are south of Crickhollow, one of the houses of the Brandybucks. If your
destination is that house, you better get there by the road. Some Brandybuck
might catch you walking around in their fields if you get too close to the
house.
name: Fields of Crickhollow

desc:
You walk around in some fields, having fun as you flatten the wheat. You would
really like to see the face of the farmer when he sees how you have made your
way through his fields.
name: Fields

desc:
There is a road both to the north and to the west. Still you choose to make you
way through the wheat of this field. The road west of you is the road between
Bucklebury and Standelf. The one north of you, will take you to the Old East
Road far north.
name: Fields

desc:
You see a long hedge going north and south separating Buckland and the Old
Forest. The hedge was built by the Brandybucks as a defence against the evil of
the Forest. It is said that a long time ago the trees of this forest attacked
Buckland.
name: The High Hay

desc:
You are in the middle of the fields of Buckland. As you walk around, you flatten
the wheat, and you guess the farmer would get very angry if he saw you here.
North of here, you see a small hobbit hole.
name: Fields

desc:
You walk through a narrow gate on a green path. You peer into an old-fashioned
countrified house. It is long and low, with no upper storey; it has a roof of
turf, round windows, and a large round door. This is Crickhollow, one of the
houses of the Brandybucks.
name: Crickhollow

desc:
You are walking in a secret passage under the hedge. It is gloomy here, and not
many hobbits have walked this passage lately. A gate is set to the east, rusting
slightly. There is no other place this passage can lead than into the dark
forest itself, you'd better be careful!
name: Tunnel under the Hedge

desc:
You are standing in the small village of Newbury, which is located just west of
the hedge. East of the hedge you can see the Old Forest, which looks threatening
and dark. West of here you can see a dusty Shire road.
name: Newbury

desc:
The Stockbrook flows from west to east between the Marish and the small hobbit
village of Stock that lies to the north. A small bridge crosses the river, and
behind the bridge the brook runs into the much larger Brandywine.
name: Stockbrook

desc:
This is a large kitchen, large enough to prepare food for the huge feasts hosted
by the Brandybucks. It seems to be kept quite tidy, at least the room is clean
and tidy right now. On some shelves a lot of dishes, cups, plates, pots and
other kitchen utensils are stored.
name: Kitchen

desc:
You enter a large smial and find yourself in a large and almost cylindric
hallway. You can enter several large rooms and halls. The size of the rooms in
here surprises you. This is the largest hobbit dwelling you have ever seen. A
large plaque is placed on one of the green walls. It can tell you about the
history of this house.
name: Inside Brandy Hall

desc:
This is a large hall, the largest room of the Brandy Hall. A large fireplace in
one of the corners makes this room comfortable on cold winter days. Several
round windows let light in from the outside. The wall is full of pictures of
important looking members of the Brandybucks. Expensive furniture tells you that
the Brandybucks are probably the most wealthy family in the Shire. Among the
pictures on the wall, you notice a drawing of a family tree.
name: A Large Hall

desc:
Judging by the sounds of ponies and cows above you, you must be under Farmer
Maggot's barn. Everywhere you look is darkness. You can feel dirt and soil
occasionally falling from the ceiling. You had better be careful because one of
those ponies or cows may come crashing down on you.
name: Under the Barn

desc:
You are inside Farmer Maggot's big stable. A ladder built into the east wall
leads up to a hayloft which extends out halfway across the stable. There are
several small stalls for the many ponies Farmer Maggot owns. Perhaps, with his
permission, you would be able to borrow one of these stout ponies for your
journey.
name: Stable

desc:
You are inside a cozy, dirt-floored barn. In the centre of the barn, there is a
small, worn out wagon. To the north is a stable. Judging from the smells coming
from the stable, you guess that there might be a pony in there.
name: Barn

desc:
You are walking in the old forest, completely lost. You have no idea where you
have walked now, and hope you can find your way out of here as quickly as
possible. You think you can see some light behind some of the trees, but they
seem to shift and move slightly, and you are just confused.
name: Lost in the Old Forest

desc:
The trees draw close on either side of you, you can no longer see far ahead.
After awhile the air around you becomes hot and stuffy. Now stronger than ever
one can feel the ill will of the forest pressing in on you. So silent it is
here, that you can barely hear the sound of your own breath, as you wander along
the chosen trail.
name: Wandering Path in the Old Forest

desc:
You are now out of the Old Forest! There is only a limited amount of trees
around you, not enough to have the strange feeling of being watched from all
around. The sky is now visible again and you appreciate this short freedom. You
take a deep breath and hope never to go through it again. Your quest was a
success, but how many persons failed before you? You can now advance proudly to
your next adventure.
name: The Border of the Old Forest

desc:
You are now out of the Old Forest! There is only a limited amount of trees
around you, not enough to have the strange feeling of being watched from all
around. The sky is now visible again and you appreciate this short freedom. You
take a deep breath and hope never to go through it again. Your quest was a
success, but how many persons failed before you? You can now advance proudly to
your next adventure.
name: The Border of the Old Forest

desc:
You are now out of the Old Forest! There is only a limited amount of trees
around you, not enough to have the strange feeling of being watched from all
around. The sky is now visible again and you appreciate this short freedom. You
take a deep breath and hope never to go through it again. Your quest was a
success, but how many persons failed before you? You can now advance proudly to
your next adventure.
name: The Border of the Old Forest

desc:
You are now out of the Old Forest! There is only a limited amount of trees
around you, not enough to have the strange feeling of being watched from all
around. The sky is now visible again and you appreciate this short freedom. You
take a deep breath and hope never to go through it again. Your quest was a
success, but how many persons failed before you? You can now advance proudly to
your next adventure.
name: The Border of the Old Forest

desc:
You are now out of the Old Forest! There is only a limited amount of trees
around you, not enough to have the strange feeling of being watched from all
around. The sky is now visible again and you appreciate this short freedom. You
take a deep breath and hope never to go through it again. Your quest was a
success, but how many persons failed before you? You can now advance proudly to
your next adventure.
name: The Border of the Old Forest

desc:
You are now out of the Old Forest! There is only a limited amount of trees
around you, not enough to have the strange feeling of being watched from all
around. The sky is now visible again and you appreciate this short freedom. You
take a deep breath and hope never to go through it again. Your quest was a
success, but how many persons failed before you? You can now advance proudly to
your next adventure.
name: The Border of the Old Forest

desc:
You are now out of the Old Forest! There is only a limited amount of trees
around you, not enough to have the strange feeling of being watched from all
around. The sky is now visible again and you appreciate this short freedom. You
take a deep breath and hope never to go through it again. Your quest was a
success, but how many persons failed before you? You can now advance proudly to
your next adventure.
name: The Border of the Old Forest

desc:
You are now out of the Old Forest! There is only a limited amount of trees
around you, not enough to have the strange feeling of being watched from all
around. The sky is now visible again and you appreciate this short freedom. You
take a deep breath and hope never to go through it again. Your quest was a
success, but how many persons failed before you? You can now advance proudly to
your next adventure.
name: The Border of the Old Forest

desc:
You are now out of the Old Forest! There is only a limited amount of trees
around you, not enough to have the strange feeling of being watched from all
around. The sky is now visible again and you appreciate this short freedom. You
take a deep breath and hope never to go through it again. Your quest was a
success, but how many persons failed before you? You can now advance proudly to
your next adventure.
name: The Border of the Old Forest

desc:
You are standing in a deep forest. The trees don't seem to be so menacing, but
you still have the sensation they are watching all your movements. The path
seems to be less obvious and you could lose yourself very easily here. You get
your map out and pray.
name: Deep inside the Old Forest

desc:
You find that you have made your way down through a cleft in a high steep bank,
almost a cliff. At its foot is a wide space of grass and reeds; and in the
distance you can see another bank almost as steep. In the midst of it winds a
lazy dark river of brown water, bordered by ancient willows.
name: Small Dark River in the Forest

desc:
You must follow the only obvious path in this huge forest. Roots spread from
everywhere and you stumble and trip often. Branches lash at your face and arms
and you find it impossible to watch your step and what lies ahead
simultaneously.
name: A Dangerous Path in the Forest

desc:
You got a glimpse of the sun in an open glade, but after a time the trees begin
to close in. They just appeared to be less dense and tangled from a distance.
Then you discover deep folds in the ground unexpectedly, like the ruts of giant
wheels or wide moats and sunken roads long disused and choked with brambles.
name: The Old Forest

desc:
This hill is so steep you must travel the slope sideways. If you tried to walk
straight up or down this hill you would gain too much momentum and would soon
tumble over and roll down this hill. Half way during your travel you notice the
faint grassy path you have been following splits into two different directions.
One of the forks leads down into what looks like a small peaceful valley.
name: Steep Hill Path

desc:
You're standing in a valley on the foot of a what seems to be the tallest hill
in the downs. To the north you see ridges of more hills that seem to fade off
into the distance. Still no sign of the road as you begin your journey again
with weary feet.
name: Valley before a Steep Hill

desc:
On top of this small hill you are able to see more clearly all the ridges you
may have to climb to the north. Also you look around for the best path to take
that might get you out of the downs a lot quicker. Clearly the best path seems
to lie to the north-east, the hills seem a little less steep in this direction.
name: On Top of a Small Hill

desc:
Steep grassy hills surround this small and secluded valley on all sides. The
only exit is to climb back up the hill that brought you to this place. The grass
under your feet is soft and lush green. The air smells so sweet from the scent
of the grass, that you decide this is a nice place to sit and relax your tired,
weary feet. On the horizon to the north you can see the rounded peaks of ridge
after ridge of hills. You've still a long way to travel to the road, so a rest
here in this beautiful valley might better prepare you physically as well as
mentally for the journey ahead of you.
name: A Tiny Valley hidden within the Hills

desc:
Before you stands what must be one of the highest hills in the downs. The only
way up the hill seems to be a faint path in the grass that wanders up the steep
incline. To your east, is a smaller hill, but much more difficult to ascend
because it's even steeper slope. South the path seems to wander to even more
hills.
name: A Grassy Path Heading up a Hill

desc:
The hills are rolling steeply here, and you have reached the end of a
well-tended path. The stone-border is sparse here, and the path goes up and
down, across the hills and between them.
name: The Well-tended Path

desc:
The path is now plain before you, well-tended and bordered with stone. It goes
up to the top of a grassy knoll, down again, and then back up a long, smooth
hillside of turf.
name: The Well-tended Path

desc:
The path is now plain before you, well-tended and bordered with stone. It goes
up to the top of a grassy knoll, down again, and then back up a long, smooth
hillside of turf.
name: The Well-tended Path

desc:
The path is now plain before you, well-tended and bordered with stone. It goes
up to the top of a grassy knoll, down again, and then back up a long, smooth
hillside of turf.
name: The Well-tended Path

desc:
The path is now plain before you, well-tended and bordered with stone. It goes
up to the top of a grassy knoll, down again, and then back up a long, smooth
hillside of turf.
name: The Well-tended Path

desc:
The path is now plain before you, well-tended and bordered with stone. It goes
up to the top of a grassy knoll, down again, and then back up a long, smooth
hillside of turf.
name: The Well-tended Path

desc:
There is no tree or visible water. It is a country of grass and short springy
turf, silent except for the whisper of the wind over the edges of the land, and
high lonely cries of strange birds. When you catch a glimpse of the country
westward the distant forest appears to be wreathed in smoke.
name: On Some Short Springy Turf

desc:
There is no tree or visible water. It is a country of grass and short springy
turf, silent except for the whisper of the wind over the edges of the land, and
high lonely cries of strange birds. When you catch a glimpse of the country
westward the distant forest appears to be wreathed in smoke.
name: On Some Short Springy Turf

desc:
Your view is screened by a tall line of beans on poles. The red flowers of the
beans glow against the green leaves. Westward, you see the impressive Old
Forest. You can hear a strange murmuring, the trees seem to communicate between
themselves. But, as you are in Tom's land, you should not fear no beast or tree.
name: A Path along the Bean Field

desc:
Your view is screened by a tall line of beans on poles. The red flowers of the
beans glow against the green leaves. Westward, you see the impressive Old
Forest. You can hear a strange murmuring, the trees seem to communicate between
themselves. But, as you are in Tom's land, you should not fear no beast or tree.
name: A Path along the Bean Field

desc:
Your view is screened by a tall line of beans on poles. The red flowers of the
beans glow against the green leaves. Westward, you see the impressive Old
Forest. You can hear a strange murmuring, the trees seem to communicate between
themselves. But, as you are in Tom's land, you should not fear no beast or tree.
name: A Path along the Bean Field

desc:
There is no tree or visible water. It is a country of grass and short springy
turf, silent except for the whisper of the wind over the edges of the land, and
high lonely cries of strange birds. When you catch a glimpse of the country
westward the distant forest appears to be wreathed in smoke.
name: On Some Short Springy Turf

desc:
There is no tree or visible water. It is a country of grass and short springy
turf, silent except for the whisper of the wind over the edges of the land, and
high lonely cries of strange birds. When you catch a glimpse of the country
westward the distant forest appears to be wreathed in smoke.
name: On Some Short Springy Turf

desc:
There is no tree or visible water. It is a country of grass and short springy
turf, silent except for the whisper of the wind over the edges of the land, and
high lonely cries of strange birds. When you catch a glimpse of the country
westward the distant forest appears to be wreathed in smoke.
name: On Some Short Springy Turf

desc:
There is no tree or visible water. It is a country of grass and short springy
turf, silent except for the whisper of the wind over the edges of the land, and
high lonely cries of strange birds. When you catch a glimpse of the country
westward the distant forest appears to be wreathed in smoke.
name: On Some Short Springy Turf

desc:
The entrance to Tom and Goldberry's house is humble. No great portal, just a
simple little gate. Flowers of every shape and colour flourish here, carefully
placed so as to best show the forms and hues of each. The clear and beautiful
voice of a maiden sings above you.
name: Tom and Goldberry's Flower Garden

desc:
The entrance to Tom and Goldberry's house is humble. No great portal, just a
simple little garden gate. You admire the work of Goldberry, the flowers are
classified by their different species. But all are flourishing, showing you
their multiple colours. You hear the clear voice of Golberry singing for the
health of her plants and you listen with delight.
name: In Front of the Garden Gate

desc:
You are on the threshold of Tom Bombadil's house. From the door to the east
comes a golden light which envelopes you. Already, half of your fears and
worries begin to disappear. From inside you can hear a clear voice, sweet as the
sound of a healthy spring playfully cascading over some nearby rocks.
name: The Threshold

desc:
The river carried you safely to this small creek. You see the river continue
smoothly further down. As you have a quick look around you, you notice a small,
well kept house. You can see some smoke coming out of the chimney. You feel that
your will has returned and you pray that these people will help you.
name: A Creek in Front of Tom's House

desc:
Just as your feet slow to a standstill, you notice that the ground is gently
rising. The water begins to murmur, and in the darkness you catch the white
glimmer of foam where the river flows over a short fall. The river continues to
the east.
name: Withywindle

desc:
Just as your feet slow to a standstill, you notice that the ground is gently
rising. The water begins to murmur, and in the darkness you catch the white
glimmer of foam where the river flows over a short fall. The river continues to
the east.
name: Withywindle

desc:
Just as your feet slow to a standstill, you notice that the ground is gently
rising. The water begins to murmur, and in the darkness you catch the white
glimmer of foam where the river flows over a short fall. The river continues to
the north.
name: Withywindle

desc:
Just as your feet slow to a standstill, you notice that the ground is gently
rising. The water begins to murmur, and in the darkness you catch the white
glimmer of foam where the river flows over a short fall. The river continues to
the east.
name: Withywindle

desc:
Just as your feet slow to a standstill, you notice that the ground is gently
rising. The water begins to murmur, and in the darkness you catch the white
glimmer of foam where the river flows over a short fall. The river continues to
the east.
name: Withywindle

desc:
Just as your feet slow to a standstill, you notice that the ground is gently
rising. The water begins to murmur, and in the darkness you catch the white
glimmer of foam where the river flows over a short fall. The river continues to
the north.
name: Withywindle

desc:
The ground seems to be gently rising with the murmur of the water. When the
light is dim, one can even catch sight of the the white glimmer of foam where
the river flows over a short fall. This river, for this part at least, seems
quite hospitable to small boats built for shallow water. It would not be
surprising to see one or more floating upon it. The river continues to the east.
name: Withywindle

desc:
You are in a river in the Forest. If it is possible to follow this river, you
might get out of the Forest eventually. It is said, however, that this river is
the centre of evil in the Forest. This river will probably take you through the
valley of Dingle, a place of great evil where several willow trees grow.
name: Withywindle

desc:
You are in a river in the Forest. If it is possible to follow this river, you
might get out of the Forest eventually. It is said, however, that this river is
the centre of evil in the Forest. This river will probably take you through the
valley of Dingle, a place of great evil where several willow trees grow.
name: Withywindle

desc:
You are in a river in the Forest. If it is possible to follow this river, you
might get out of the Forest eventually. It is said, however, that this river is
the centre of evil in the Forest. This river will probably take you through the
valley of Dingle, a place of great evil where several willow trees grow.
name: Withywindle

to close in. They just appeared to be less dense and tangled from a distance.
Then you discover deep folds in the ground unexpectedly, like the ruts of giant
wheels or wide moats and sunken roads long disused and choked with brambles.
name: The Old Forest

Then you discover deep folds in the ground unexpectedly, like the ruts of giant
wheels or wide moats and sunken roads long disused and choked with brambles.
name: The Old Forest

Then you discover deep folds in the ground unexpectedly, like the ruts of giant
wheels or wide moats and sunken roads long disused and choked with brambles.
name: The Old Forest

desc:
You got a glimpse of the sun in an open glade, but after a time the trees begin
to close in. They just appeared to be less dense and tangled from a distance.
Then you discover deep folds in the ground unexpectedly, like the ruts of giant
wheels or wide moats and sunken roads long disused and choked with brambles.
name: The Old Forest

desc:
You got a glimpse of the sun in an open glade, but after a time the trees begin
to close in. They just appeared to be less dense and tangled from a distance.
Then you discover deep folds in the ground unexpectedly, like the ruts of giant
wheels or wide moats and sunken roads long disused and choked with brambles.
name: The Old Forest

desc:
You are walking in tall grass where you can see the tracks of many animals,
especially of the ones you enjoyed with a pint of good beer when you last ate in
your village inn. But, do you dare disturb the peace of this place?
name: The Hunting Place

desc:
You are walking in tall grass where you can see the tracks of many animals.
Eastwards you see the hills rolling softly into a mist.
name: The Hunting Place

desc:
You are walking in tall grass where you can see the tracks of many animals,
especially of the ones you enjoyed with a pint of good beer when you last ate in
your village inn. But, do you dare disturb the peace of this place?
name: The Hunting Place

desc:
You are walking in tall grass where you can see the tracks of many animals,
especially of the ones you enjoyed with a pint of good beer when you last ate in
your village inn. But, do you dare disturb the peace of this place?
name: The Hunting Place

desc:
All of the ground is covered with hay, but you ought to watch where you put your
feet.
name: The Pony Stall

desc:
The entrance to Tom and Goldberry's house is humble. No great portal, just a
simple little garden gate. You admire the work of Goldberry, the flowers are
classified by their diffrent species. But all are flourishing, showing you their
multiple colors. You hear the clear voice of Golberry singing for the health of
her plants and you listen with delight.
name: Tom and Goldberry's Flower Garden

desc:
The entrance to Tom and Goldberry's house is humble. No great portal, just a
simple little garden gate. You admire the work of Goldberry, the flowers are
classified by their diffrent species. But all are flourishing, showing you their
multiple colors. You hear the clear voice of Goldberry singing for the health of
her plants and you listen with delight.
name: Tom and Goldberry's Flower Garden

desc:
Just as your feet slow to a standstill, you notice that the ground is gently
rising. The water begins to murmur, and in the darkness you catch the white
glimmer of foam where the river flows over a short fall. The river continues to
the east.
name: Withywindle

desc:
Off to the north you can see the beginning of Tom Bombadil's lovely flower
garden. A number of flowers grow here as well, but most of the garden is north
of here. The threshold of Tom's house lies to the south.
name: The Entrance to the Flower Garden

desc:
The kitchen is a small room with a fire place and a big wooden table. You can
imagine Goldberry cooking blackberry tarts or other fine desserts, good as can
be found in the Shire.
name: The Kitchen

desc:
You are in a long, low room, filled with the light of lamps swinging from the
beams of the ceiling. A magnificent chair, surrounded by white water-lilies
preserved in green and brown earthenware, seems to be awaiting its noble
occupant.
name: The Main Room

desc:
You go down a short staircase and find yourself in a low room with a sloping
ceiling. Its walls are of clean stone, but they are mostly covered with green
and yellow curtains. There are four deep mattresses, each piled with white
blankets.
name: The Low Bedroom

desc:
You enter a bathroom and see a long bench placed against the wall with a wide
earthenware basin. Beside it stand brown ewers filled with water, some cold,
some steaming hot.
name: The Bathroom

desc:
This is a large bedroom in the house of Tom Bombadil, easily able to accomodate
six people. Several beds are made and neatly cared for, ready for any visitors.
To the north the bathroom can be entered, and a doorway to the west leads into
the main room.
name: The Large Bedroom

desc:
The fields end here. To the south is a hedge bending north to the east and south
to the west. Behind it you see a dark forest. There are large fields north of
you.
name: Fields

desc:
This is the famous Golden Perch Inn, where all hobbits long to go. They serve
the best beer in the East Farthing, maybe you should have a taste? This seems to
be a favoured meeting place, and you hear all sorts of stories being told by the
merry hobbits. A sign is attached to the wall here.
name: The Golden Perch

desc:
You are in the southern part of Stock. To the west you see a small house, and a
little shop is to the east. You hear the sounds of hobbits doing various
activities all around you, and you can't avoid hearing about the Golden Perch,
the famous inn.
name: South End of Stock

desc:
You are standing in a nice and homey shop that supplies the people of Stock and
those of the surrounding countryside with the basic necessities of life. You see
a small cheerful halfling shopkeeper standing behind a small oaken counter. He
smiles at you and you feel at ease to browse through his wares. A sign rests on
the counter.
name: The Shop

desc:
You are standing in a small building which apparently is some kind of office for
the Shirriffs. Judging by the looks of this, the Shirriffs in the Shire are more
adept at tea-drinking than fighting, and you guess that most of the Shiriffs
would rather hang out in the inn.
name: Shirriffs' Office

desc:
You enter a large, neat foyer. There is a hook on the wall for you to hang your
coat on. The room is a bit dirty from all the weary hobbits who forgot to wipe
the dust from outside off of their feet.
name: The Zarboff Smial

desc:
The path traverses in an almost straight line through the peaceful landscape,
widening a little to the east, where the village of Stock can be glimpsed. North
of here a large field opens up, and to the west the path goes on.
name: Path to Stock

desc:
This is the Zarboff family living room. There is a table in the centre of the
room, and couches lining each wall. It is a rather large room, and could hold
about twenty hobbits, and on some occasions, it does! The room smells of mauve
and pipeweed smoke.
name: A Cozy Living Room

desc:
There are all sorts of things lying in unorganized heaps on the ground of this
cozy bedroom. An unmade bed sits in one corner of the room, its blankets
scattered all over.
name: Master Zippo's Bedroom

desc:
You are in the westernmost part of Buckland, in the middle of large fields.
About a league north of you, you can see the small village of Standelf. Far away
in the west, you see another. You guess the latter must be Rushey. It would be
easier to get there if you travelled on a road.
name: Fields

desc:
Somewhat unusual for Hobbits, this dwelling is above ground. Framed with solid
timber and built with tight stonework, this cottage is seemingly very well
constructed. The air is thick with herbs and spices, giving the room a
distinctly medicinal smell. A large cooking fireplace takes up one corner of the
room with a massive copper cauldron slowly boiling some kind of chunky stew.
Several tables and cupboards in the room contain a large collection of spices,
liquids, and all manner of vegetables.The most striking feature of this tidy
home is a large, iron-banded trapdoor set into the middle of the earthen floor.
name: Earthen Cottage

desc:
A set of beautiful wooden stairs leads down into a large, well-appointed smial.
The earthen walls are rubbed completely smooth, appearing as if made from a
rich, polished dark-brown wood. There are several round windows that overlook
the Brandywine that light the room and fill it with the pleasant sound of
rushing water. Well-appointed furniture, luxurious carpets, and plush pillows
fill the room. A large wooden table dominates the center the space and is
covered with all manner of pastries, cheeses, meats, and other mouthwatering
delicacies. Innumerable mathoms, pictures, mementos, and other ephemera fill
large shelves against the walls. A tastefully molded corridor continues west
towards another room.
name: Well-appointed Smial

desc:
You are walking in the old forest, completely lost. You have no idea where you
have walked now, and hope you can find your way out of here as quickly as
possible. You think you can see some light behind some of the trees, but they
seem to shift and move slightly, and you are just confused.
name: Lost in the Old Forest

desc:
You are walking in the old forest, completely lost. You have no idea where you
have walked now, and hope you can find your way out of here as quickly as
possible. You think you can see some light behind some of the trees, but they
seem to shift and move slightly, and you are just confused.
name: Lost in the Old Forest

desc:
You seem to be trapped in the forest! In some mysterious way the trees just
closed the way back to safety, you have no idea where you are now. The only way
to go seems to be further into the forest. The trees creak slowly with strange
sounds, and you think you can sense something evil behind all this.
name: The Old Forest

desc:
You are walking in the old forest, completely lost. You have no idea where you
have walked now, and hope you can find your way out of here as quickly as
possible. You think you can see some light behind some of the trees, but they
seem to shift and move slightly, and you are just confused.
name: Lost in the Old Forest

desc:
You have reached another point in the path where you feel the trees once again
are forcing you to make a choice. You feel the ill will of the trees become
stronger at this point. You begin to realise that getting out of this wood will
be no easy task to achieve. To the south you feel the animosity is stronger
towards you than the path to the north. Maybe going north would be much safer?
name: Wandering Path in the Old Forest

desc:
Still you feel the intent gaze of the trees following your every movement. You
hear soft laughter from above, as they watch you wander around hopelessly lost
within their realm. You feel the ever growing sense of ill will from the trees,
as you try to make your way through this wood. Every once in a while, you'll
hear movement behind you, but as you turn to gaze, there is nothing there. The
only thing you do know, is that you want out of here, and you want out fast.
name: Wandering Path in the Old Forest

desc:
Still you feel the intent gaze of the trees following your every movement. You
hear soft laughter from above, as they watch you wander around hopelessly lost
within their realm. You feel the ever growing sense of ill will from the trees,
as you try to make your way through this wood. Every once in a while, you'll
hear movement behind you, but as you turn to gaze, there is nothing there. The
only thing you do know, is that you want out of here, and you want out fast.
name: Wandering Path in the Old Forest

desc:
Still you feel the intent gaze of the trees following your every movement. You
hear soft laughter from above, as they watch you wander around hopelessly lost
within their realm. You feel the ever growing sense of ill will from the trees,
as you try to make your way through this wood. Every once in a while, you'll
hear movement behind you, but as you turn to gaze, there is nothing there. The
only thing you do know, is that you want out of here, and you want out fast.
name: Wandering Path in the Old Forest

desc:
Still you feel the intent gaze of the trees following your every movement. You
hear soft laughter from above, as they watch you wander around hopelessly lost
within their realm. You feel the ever growing sense of ill will from the trees,
as you try to make your way through this wood. Every once in a while, you'll
hear movement behind you, but as you turn to gaze, there is nothing there. The
only thing you do know, is that you want out of here, and you want out fast.
name: Wandering Path in the Old Forest

desc:
Still you feel the intent gaze of the trees following your every movement. You
hear soft laughter from above, as they watch you wander around hopelessly lost
within their realm. You feel the ever growing sense of ill will from the trees,
as you try to make your way through this wood. Every once in awhile, you'll hear
movement behind you, but as you turn to gaze, there is nothing there. The only
thing you do know, is that you want out of here, and you want out fast.
name: Wandering Path in the Old Forest

desc:
You are standing on another small path. This one seems to be identical to the
other ones, except that this one is less chaotic. You can walk with less pain
but you still feel the many wounds inflicted by the last path you decided to
take. Each step further seems to lead you to a final issue... Will it be death
or survival?
name: Wandering Path in the Old Forest

desc:
You are standing on another small path. This one seems to be identical to the
other ones, except that this one is less chaotic. You can walk with less pain
but you still feel the many wounds inflicted by the last path you decided to
take. Each step further seems to lead you to a final issue... Will it be death
or survival?
name: Wandering Path in the Old Forest

desc:
You are standing on another small path. This one seems to be identical to the
other ones, except that this one is less chaotic. You can walk with less pain
but you still feel the many wounds inflicted by the last path you decided to
take. Each step further seems to lead you to a final issue... Will it be death
or survival?
name: Wandering Path in the Old Forest

desc:
You are standing on another small path. This one seems to be identical to the
other ones, except that this one is less chaotic. You can walk with less pain
but you still feel the many wounds inflicted by the last path you decided to
take. Each step further seems to lead you to a final issue... Will it be death
or survival?
name: Wandering Path in the Old Forest

desc:
You are standing on another small path. This one seems to be identical to the
other ones, except that this one is less chaotic. You can walk with less pain
but you still feel the many wounds inflicted by the last path you decided to
take. Each step further seems to lead you to a final issue... Will it be death
or survival?
name: Wandering Path in the Old Forest

desc:
Once again you're on a single wandering path that seems chosen for you. Though
now you feel as if the trees are forcing you to roam around in circles. Or is
that you've seen so many trees they all begin to look the same? You hear
definite laughter coming from above, and taunting. You begin to wonder when
you'll ever get out of this wood.
name: Wandering Path in the Old Forest

desc:
Once again you're on a single wandering path that seems chosen for you. Though
now you feel as if the trees are forcing you to roam around in circles. Or is
that you've seen so many trees they all begin to look the same? You hear
definite laughter coming from above, and taunting. You begin to wonder when
you'll ever get out of this wood.
name: Wandering Path in the Old Forest

desc:
Once again you're on a single wandering path that seems chosen for you. Though
now you feel as if the trees are forcing you to roam around in circles. Or is
that you've seen so many trees they all begin to look the same? You hear
definite laughter coming from above, and taunting. You begin to wonder when
you'll ever get out of this wood.
name: Wandering Path in the Old Forest

desc:
Once again you're on a single wandering path that seems chosen for you. Though
now you feel as if the trees are forcing you to roam around in circles. Or is
that you've seen so many trees they all begin to look the same? You hear
definite laughter coming from above, and taunting. You begin to wonder when
you'll ever get out of this wood.
name: Wandering Path in the Old Forest

desc:
You've reached a fork in the path. You can go east or south at this point. You
feel as if the trees are forcing you to choose a direction. You wonder why they
offer you a choice, you'll ultimately go where the trees want you to anyway.
name: Wandering Path in the Old Forest

desc:
This path has lead you to what appears to be a dead end. Seems as if the Old
Forest has tricked you into choosing this path. The only exit is the one that
has brought you here. There is a huge, menacing looking ancient tree standing
here as if frozen.
name: Path in the North part of the Old Forest

desc:
This appears to be a continuation of a single wandering path. It seems as if the
trees here are forcing most travellers to roam around in circles or perhaps it
is just that so many of the trees look the same. Often times the sound of a
taunting laughter can be heard coming from some source above.
name: Wandering Path in the Old Forest

desc:
You're standing in a small room of a hollowed out tree trunk. There seems to be
no purpose for its use. Since the tree was frozen long ago, the inside of the
base of the trunk has rotted out and left this small but uneven room. It is warm
here, and seems quite safe as long as you keep the exit closed. You begin to
wonder who has built this room and why, it seems to be of a time long ago...
name: Inside of an Ancient Tree

desc:
Once again you're on a single wandering path that seems chosen for you. Though
now you feel as if the trees are forcing you to roam around in circles. Or is
that you've seen so many trees they all begin to look the same? You hear
definite laughter coming from above, and taunting. You begin to wonder when
you'll ever get out of this wood.
name: Wandering Path in the Old Forest

desc:
Once again you're on a single wandering path that seems chosen for you. Though
now you feel as if the trees are forcing you to roam around in circles. Or is
that you've seen so many trees they all begin to look the same? You hear
definite laughter coming from above, and taunting. You begin to wonder when
you'll ever get out of this wood.
name: Wandering Path in the Old Forest

desc:
You are standing on another small path. This one seems to be identical to the
other ones, except that this one is less chaotic. You can walk with less pain
but you still feel the many wounds inflicted by the last path you decided to
take. Each step further seems to lead you to a final issue... Will it be death
or survival?
name: Wandering Path in the Old Forest

desc:
You are standing in a deep forest. The trees don't seem to be so menacing, but
you still have the sensation they are watching all your movements. The path
seems to be less obvious and you could lose yourself very easily here. You get
your map out and pray.
name: Deep inside the Old Forest

desc:
You find that you have made your way down through a cleft in a high steep bank,
almost a cliff. At its foot is a wide space of grass and reeds, and in the
distance you can see another bank almost as steep. In the midst of it winds a
lazy dark river of brown water, bordered by ancient willows.
name: Small Dark River in the Forest

desc:
You are standing in a deep forest. The trees don't seem to be so menacing, but
you still have the sensation they are watching all your movements. The path
seems to be less obvious and you could lose yourself very easily here. You get
your map out and pray.

almost a cliff. At its foot is a wide space of grass and reeds, and in the
distance you can see another bank almost as steep. In the midst of it winds a

name: Small Dark River in the Forest

desc:
This is the western border of the Old Forest. The larger trees seem as if they
are aware of all that goes on under their branches. Perhaps watching and waiting
to act should something they see disturb them. To the west is the High Hay, a
hedge the protects Buckland by forming an impassable boundary between the forest
and the town.
name: Old Forest

desc:
This is the Old Forest. The huge trees look really old like remains of the long
gone times when forests like this covered all Eriador. Old tales say that the
trees of this forest move around and plot schemes against intruders and that
they hate every being walking on two legs. The High Hay marks the western border
of the forest and prevents all travel west.
name: Old Forest

desc:
This is the Old Forest, a remnant of the great forest that once covered most of
Eriador. The trees have an odd quality about them. It is as if they seem to be
moving slowly and watching every move. Perhaps though, it is just the wind
whispering in the leaves and not that the trees are whispering schemes against
those who dare to walk under them.
name: Old Forest

desc:
This is the Old Forest, a remnant of the great forest that once covered most of
Eriador. The trees have an odd quality about them. It is as if they seem to be
moving slowly and watching every move. Perhaps though, it is just the wind
whispering in the leaves and not that the trees are whispering schemes against

whispering in the leaves and not that the trees are whispering schemes against
those who dare to walk under them.
name: Old Forest

desc:
This is the Old Forest. The trees of this very ancient forest are said to be
somehow odd. It is said that they would move around and open and close paths. A
clearing seems to be west from here.
name: Old Forest

those who dare to walk under them. From a small hill Buckland can be seen not

name: Old Forest

desc:

name: Old Forest

desc:
This is the Old Forest, a remnant of the great forest that once covered most of
Eriador. You are completely lost in here. The trees seem to be moving slowly,
and you feel that they watch your moves. You are now very deep into the forest.
You hear a faint sound of running water here, but you can't see its origin.
name: Old Forest

desc:
This is the place where the Hobbits of Buckland burned so many trees during the
attack of the Forest on Buckland. No trees have grown here since that time, but
grass, weeds and nettles are scattered about the glade, and the Old Forest looms
at the edge of the clearing, gloomy and forbidding.
name: Bonfire Glade

desc:
This is the Old Forest, a remnant of the great forest that once covered most of
Eriador. You are completely lost in here. The trees seem to be moving slowly,

was a bad idea to enter this forest. Will you ever get out of it?

desc:
The Ferry feels a bit unsteady to walk on as you board it, but it soon feels
steady, and you know that it will take you safely across the Brandywine river.
The river floats very slowly and silently at this point.
name: Bucklebury Ferry
